§6Fullerene Solution§f

§aA corrosive chemical fluid used for SILEX isotope separation — the key to processing exotic materials!§f

§bFullerene Solution§f is a strongly corrosive, polluting liquid (NFPA 3 health/reactivity) and the base fluid for fullerene production. It is hard to obtain in large quantities as it requires §bHigh-Performance Solvent§f, a late mid-game fluid, and §bFine Soot§f produced from mitigating pollution.

§6Production§f (§bIndustrial Mixer§f):
§b• 500 mB§f High-Performance Solvent
§b• 1×§f Fine Soot
§b→ §e250 mB§f Fullerene Solution

§6Key Use§f: §bSILEX§f
Fullerene Solution is used in the SILEX (Separation of Isotopes by Laser Excitation) process with a Bismuth laser crystal to produce §eFullerene§f at 100% efficiency.

§eTip§f: Stockpile high-performance solvent and automate fine soot production (via Industrial Smokestack pollution mitigation or Pyrolysis Oven) before scaling up. The 2:1 input-to-output ratio means you need significant infrastructure to produce fullerenes in bulk!
